title: nymphaea alba subsp. tetragona
intro: Nymphaea alba subsp. tetragona, also known as the white water lily or Egyptian white water lily, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Nymphaea genus. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Nymphaea alba subsp. tetragona include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and treating various infections, as well as exhibiting potential neuroprotective effects. Herbal preparations of this plant include tea, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
